goal: Registration stops being a no-op — it persists a workshop row tied to the signed-in mechanic and stores the certificate in the private workshop-certs bucket, with the path saved to workshops.cert_path and the success screen reflecting the persisted values.
why: Registration is the last dead-end flow (RegisterForm.tsx:43 — setSubmitted(true); // frontend-only); closing it makes the demo's 'real over impressive' promise true on every flow and exercises the private-bucket + signed-URL path no other flow touches.
success_criteria: Submitting the registration form as the seeded mechanic creates one workshops row with that mechanic's owner_id, the entered name, and serial.
The certificate file lands in the private workshop-certs bucket under {uid}/cert.<ext> and workshops.cert_path holds that exact path.
The success screen shows the persisted name/serial/file and a working 'View certificate' link backed by createSignedUrl (not a client object URL).
Server-side validation rejects an oversized or wrong-type file and an unauthenticated/non-mechanic caller with a calm bilingual message — no partial write, no raw error code.
EL and EN render on every new string; a missing translation fails the build.

tasks.title: Registration server action: validate, upload cert, persist workshop
acceptance_criteria: A valid name + serial + image/PDF file <= 5MB creates exactly one workshops row whose owner_id equals the signed-in mechanic's uid, with the provided name and serial.
The certificate uploads to workshop-certs under {uid}/cert.<ext> with upsert:true, and that exact path is written to workshops.cert_path.
The action returns a typed success result carrying name, serial, certPath, and a signedUrl (createSignedUrl valid >= 5 minutes).
An unauthenticated caller, a non-mechanic role, an oversized file (>5MB), or a wrong MIME type each return a typed error result with a stable code — never an unhandled throw and never a partial row.
tasks.action: Create src/lib/actions/register.ts with a top-level use-server directive (npm i zod if absent). registerSchema validates name/serial and the cert File (reject >5MB or non image/PDF MIME). registerWorkshop awaits createClient(), checks auth then role==mechanic, parses FormData to typed invalid_* codes (no throw), uploads {uid}/cert.<ext> upsert:true, upserts the workshops row (onConflict owner_id) with cert_path, calls createSignedUrl(path,300), returns RegisterResult. Never imports service_role.

tasks.title: Wire RegisterForm to the action: real submit, persisted success screen, bilingual states
acceptance_criteria: Submitting a valid registration calls registerWorkshop, and on success the screen shows the persisted name, serial, and certificate filename plus a 'View certificate' link that opens the signed URL.
While the action is in flight the submit button shows a submitting label and is disabled; on ok:false an inline bilingual error (mapped from result.code) appears and the form stays filled.
A file rejected by the server (too large / wrong type) surfaces the invalid_file message in the current language; an oversized file does not appear 'saved'.
Every new string is present in BOTH el and en in lib/i18n.ts — a missing key is a TypeScript build error.
tasks.action: Extend i18n.ts Translation + both el/en with submitting, viewCert, regErrors (one calm EL/EN line per error code). In RegisterForm.tsx call registerWorkshop with FormData(name,serial,cert) via useActionState tracking pending: button shows t.submitting/disabled; ok:false shows inline text-negative t.regErrors[code] keeping fields; ok:true sources success rows from result + a t.viewCert anchor to result.signedUrl. Remove the frontend-only no-op. No raw hex, no native select.
